steps.prompt: Which word is the finite verb in the first clause?

hints.pater: 'Pater' is a noun meaning 'father' — the subject
hints.illum: 'Illum' is a demonstrative pronoun meaning 'him' in the accusative
hints.motus: 'Motus' is a perfect passive participle — part of the second verb phrase

hints.present indicative: Present would be 'videt' — 'Vidit' has the perfect stem 'vid-'

hints.vinco, vincere, vici, victum: These are the parts of 'vincere' (to conquer)
hints.vivo, vivere, vixi, victum: These are the parts of 'vivere' (to live)
hints.vito, vitare, vitavi, vitatum: These are the parts of 'vitare' (to avoid)

hints.present active participle: A present active participle would be 'movens' — 'motus' ends in -tus
hints.future active participle: A future active participle would be 'moturus' — 'motus' is perfect passive
hints.gerundive: A gerundive would be 'movendus' — 'motus' is a completed passive action

hints.present passive indicative: Present passive would be 'movetur' — 'motus est' uses the participle
hints.pluperfect passive indicative: Pluperfect passive would be 'motus erat' — 'est' makes this perfect
hints.imperfect passive indicative: Imperfect passive would be 'movebatur' — 'motus est' is perfect

hints.moneo, monere, monui, monitum: These are the parts of 'monere' (to warn/advise)
hints.morior, mori, mortuus sum, —: These are the parts of 'mori' (to die)
hints.mitto, mittere, misi, missum: These are the parts of 'mittere' (to send)

hints.nominative: 'Misericordia' could be nominative, but here it expresses the cause of being moved — ablative
hints.accusative: Accusative would be 'misericordiam' — 'misericordia' is ablative here
hints.vocative: One does not address 'compassion' — 'misericordia' is ablative of cause

hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'misericordiae' ends in -ae
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'misericordiae' ends in -ae
hints.5th declension: 5th declension has genitive in -ei — 'misericordiae' ends in -ae

translation: His father saw him and was moved with compassion
